reasoning: The Lean signature captures all three parts of the informal statement precisely: `[MetricSpace X]` encodes "metric space"; `[ConnectedSpace X]` encodes "connected" (and in Mathlib ConnectedSpace already implies Nonempty, but the informal "more than one point" hypothesis is stronger and explicitly given via `∃ x y : X, x ≠ y`); and the conclusion `¬ Countable (univ : Set X)` is equivalent to X being uncountable — via `Set.CoeSort`, `Countable (univ : Set X)` elaborates as `Countable ↥univ`, and `countable_univ_iff` in Mathlib states this is equivalent to `Countable X`. So the negation is exactly the claim "X is uncountable". No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure matches. The formalization is strictly faithful.
